# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @products = Product.all

    if search_params.present?
      @products = filter_by_name(@products)
      @products = filter_by_category(@products)
      @products = filter_by_price_range(@products)
      @products = apply_sorting(@products)
    end

    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 20)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render :search }
      format.json { render json: @products, meta: pagination_meta(@products) }
    end
  end

  private

  def search_params
    params.fetch(:search, {}).permit(
      :name,
      :category,
      :category_id,
      :min_price,
      :max_price,
      :sort_by,
      :sort_direction,
      :in_stock,
      :brand,
      tags: []
    )
  end

  def filter_by_name(scope)
    return scope if search_params[:name].blank?

    # Safe parameterized query — NOT string interpolation
    scope.where("products.name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:name])}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(scope)
    if search_params[:category_id].present?
      scope.where(category_id: search_params[:category_id])
    elsif search_params[:category].present?
      scope.joins(:category).where(categories: { name: search_params[:category] })
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(scope)
    if search_params[:min_price].present? && search_params[:max_price].present?
      min = BigDecimal(search_params[:min_price].to_s)
      max = BigDecimal(search_params[:max_price].to_s)
      scope.where(price: min..max)
    elsif search_params[:min_price].present?
      min = BigDecimal(search_params[:min_price].to_s)
      scope.where("products.price >= ?", min)
    elsif search_params[:max_price].present?
      max = BigDecimal(search_params[:max_price].to_s)
      scope.where("products.price <= ?", max)
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  def apply_sorting(scope)
    allowed_sort_columns = %w[name price created_at updated_at]
    allowed_directions = %w[asc desc]

    sort_by = search_params[:sort_by].presence_in(allowed_sort_columns) || "created_at"
    sort_direction = search_params[:sort_direction].presence_in(allowed_directions) || "desc"

    scope.order("products.#{sort_by}" => sort_direction.to_sym)
  end

  # Rails' built-in helper to escape LIKE wildcards (%, _)
  def sanitize_sql_like(string)
    ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(string)
  end

  def pagination_meta(collection)
    {
      current_page: collection.current_page,
      total_pages: collection.total_pages,
      total_count: collection.total_count,
      per_page: collection.limit_value
    }
  end
end
